inclusion.I1: English language empirical studies with original data
inclusion.I2: Functional MRI scan conducted while human participants assessing drug or natural reward-related cue-reactivity using visual stimuli

exclusion.E2: Cue-reactivity using only other sensory cues (e.g., gustatory, olfactory, tactile)

inclusion.I3: Functional MRI study assesing drug or natural reward-related cue-reactivity using visual stimuli
inclusion.I4: At least one analysis involving a within-participant contrast of drug cues>control stimuli or natural reward-related cues>control stimuli
inclusion.I5: Whole-brain evidence- At least one analysis must be a group-level, whole-brain (voxelwise) task-evoked statistical map
inclusion.I6: It is acceptable for qualifying whole-brain results to be reported in tables of coordinates, figures, or supplementary materials, as long as they are clearly described in the text.
inclusion.I7: Relevant information was reported regarding participant age and sex, cue type (i.e., nicotine, alcohol, cannabis, cocaine, heroin, food, or sexual), MRI scanner field strength, and processing software (e.g., AFNI, FSL, SPM).

exclusion.E3: Studies that report only region-of-interest (ROI) analyses with no whole-brain results.
exclusion.E4: Studies that report only seed-based connectivity, effective connectivity (e.g., PPI, DCM), or resting-state analyses (e.g., ICA, PLE, Hurst exponents) without also reporting a whole-brain task activation map.
exclusion.E5: Studies that report only between-group contrasts or interactions (e.g., subgroup vs subgroup, patient vs control) without an accompanying unmasked, whole-brain task effect.
exclusion.E6: Non-empirical papers (e.g., reviews, meta-analyses, methods-only, simulations) without original fMRI data in healthy adults.

inclusion.GLOBAL_I1: Whole-brain evidence- The analysis must be from a group-level, univariate whole-brain (voxelwise) task-evoked statistical map (e.g., main effect or within-group contrast) that is from the healthy/control group and is generalizable to the healthy adult population.
inclusion.GLOBAL_I2: Contrast or analysis must be relevant to drug or natural reward-related cue-reactivity using visual stimuli (e.g., drug cues>neutral cues, sexual cues>neutral cues, food cues>neutral cues).

exclusion.GLOBAL_E2: Seed-based connectivity, effective connectivity (e.g., PPI, DCM), or resting-state analyses (e.g., ICA, PLE, Hurst exponents)
exclusion.GLOBAL_E3: Between-group contrasts or interactions (e.g., subgroup vs subgroup, patient vs control)

inclusion.POOLED_CUE_REACTIVITY_I1: Analyses must be on a task measuring drug or natural reward-related cue-reactivity using visual stimuli (example tasks: Drug cue reactivity; Food cue reactivity; Sexual cue reactivity)
inclusion.POOLED_CUE_REACTIVITY_I2: The analysis/contrast must measure drug or natural reward-related cue reactivity compared to control stimuli (example contrast: Drug cues>neutral cues; Food cues>neutral cues; Sexual cues>neutral cues)

inclusion.NATURAL_REWARD_CUE_REACTIVITY_I1: Analyses must be on a task measuring natural reward-related cue-reactivity (example tasks: Food cue reactivity; Social cue reactivity, Sexual cue reactivity)
inclusion.NATURAL_REWARD_CUE_REACTIVITY_I2: The analysis/contrast must measure natural reward-related cue reactivity compared to control stimuli (example contrast: Food cues>neutral cues; Social cues>neutral cues; Sexual cues>neutral cues)

exclusion.NATURAL_REWARD_CUE_REACTIVITY_E1: Drug cue contrasts (e.g., Drug cues>neutral cues) should not be included in this annotation

inclusion.DRUG_CUE_REACTIVITY_I1: Analyses must be on a task measuring drug-related cue-reactivity (example tasks: Drug cue reactivity)
inclusion.DRUG_CUE_REACTIVITY_I2: The analysis/contrast must measure drug-related cue reactivity compared to control stimuli (example contrast: Drug cues>neutral cues)

exclusion.DRUG_CUE_REACTIVITY_E1: Analyses on natural reward-related cue contrasts (e.g., Food cues>neutral cues) should not be included in this annotation
